untranslatable_reason: Double Bottom pattern requires identifying two distinct local minima at similar price levels and tracking a partial recovery between them. This requires multi-bar pattern recognition (lookback window for first bottom, recovery high, second bottom identification) which cannot be expressed with single-bar indicator comparisons. The signal also references 'confirmation point' and 'breakout price' concepts that require relative price tracking across multiple bars without explicit bar indexing support.
translation_notes: Signal describes a reversal pattern requiring: (1) identification of first bottom, (2) partial recovery, (3) second bottom formation near first bottom level, (4) breakout above confirmation point (10% above second bottom). The 33% success rate and 64% failure rate at 5% suggest high false positive rate. Entry attempted as simple breakout condition (close rising 10%), exit as pullback below previous low. However, true pattern detection would require custom multi-bar pattern logic beyond allowed indicators/operators.
